repair_without_defensiveness.title: Repair Without Defensiveness
repair_without_defensiveness.purpose: Own impact before explaining intent; restore safety and accountability.
steps: Pause and soften tone/body (stop escalation).
Name what you did (specific behavior; no excuses).
Acknowledge impact (reflect what you heard).
Take ownership (no counter-accusations).
Ask what repair would help right now.
Commit to one concrete change + a follow-up time.
micro_script: You’re right. I did ___.
I can see how that impacted you as ___.
I’m not going to defend it. I own it.
What would help you feel safer right now?
Next time I will ___. I’ll follow up by ___.

closure_transition_protocol.title: Closure + Transition Protocol
closure_transition_protocol.purpose: Prevent abandonment/stonewalling signals by closing loops and giving a clear next timepoint.
steps: Name the pause/exit explicitly (no silent disappearance).
Give a one-sentence reason (capacity/safety; not blame).
State what is still open (what you are not abandoning).
Give a specific reconnection time or condition.
Follow up with a brief message if leaving physically or going offline.
micro_script: I need to pause for __ minutes to regulate.
I’m not abandoning this. The open issue is ___.
I will come back at __ and continue.
If that changes, I will text you immediately with the new time.

reality_repair_protocol.title: Reality Repair Protocol
reality_repair_protocol.purpose: Repair reality distortion by clarifying facts, admitting uncertainty, and restoring trust.
steps: Stop attacking the other person’s perception.
State your memory as your memory (not as the only truth).
Validate that their experience is real to them.
Separate facts from interpretations; list agreed facts.
Admit uncertainty where needed; propose verification method (notes/messages).
Commit to truth + correction if you were wrong.
micro_script: Here’s what I remember: ___.
I believe you experienced it as ___. I’m not attacking your perception.
Facts we can agree on: ___.
If I’m wrong, I will correct it and own the impact.

impact_acknowledgement_protocol.title: Impact Acknowledgement Protocol
impact_acknowledgement_protocol.purpose: Repair minimization by taking the impact seriously and responding proportionally.
steps: Repeat back the impact you heard (their words).
Confirm the impact matters (no downplaying).
Apologize for the impact (not for being misunderstood).
Ask what part hurt most (one clarifying question).
Offer one repair action now and one prevention action later.
micro_script: I hear the impact was ___.
That matters. I’m sorry I caused that.
What part hurt the most?
Right now I can ___. Next time I will ___.

listening_repair_protocol.title: Listening Repair Protocol
listening_repair_protocol.purpose: Repair dismissal/interruptions by restoring turn-taking, reflection, and accuracy.
steps: Stop interrupting and let them finish.
Reflect back what you heard (summary + emotion).
Ask if your reflection is accurate.
Ask one curiosity question before responding.
Respond only after confirmation.
micro_script: Let me slow down and listen.
What I heard is ___. Did I get that right?
What would you want me to understand most?

validation_repair_protocol.title: Validation Repair Protocol
validation_repair_protocol.purpose: Repair invalidation by acknowledging emotion and making space for experience.
steps: Name the emotion you hear.
Acknowledge it makes sense given their perspective.
Do not correct the feeling or argue the emotion.
Ask what support is wanted (space, listening, solution, reassurance).
Confirm what you will do next.
micro_script: It sounds like you feel ___.
That makes sense given ___.
Do you want listening, space, or a plan?

respect_repair_protocol.title: Respect Repair Protocol
respect_repair_protocol.purpose: Repair contempt/mockery by restoring dignity and respectful tone.
steps: Stop sarcasm/mockery immediately.
Name the disrespectful behavior plainly.
Apologize for tone/action and impact.
Restate your point respectfully (or pause if escalated).
Commit to respectful speech in the next exchange.
micro_script: My tone was disrespectful. I’m sorry.
I can see it landed as ___.
Let me restate this respectfully: ___.

autonomy_repair_protocol.title: Autonomy Repair Protocol
autonomy_repair_protocol.purpose: Repair pressure/manipulation by restoring freedom and consent.
steps: Name the pressure that was applied.
Remove the leverage (guilt/fear consequence framing).
Explicitly affirm their freedom to say no.
Ask what they prefer without pushing.
Accept the answer without penalty.
micro_script: I pressured you. That wasn’t fair.
You’re free to say no.
What do you prefer?

promise_repair_protocol.title: Promise Repair Protocol
promise_repair_protocol.purpose: Repair broken commitments by naming breach, restoring clarity, and creating follow-through.
steps: Name the specific promise that was broken.
Acknowledge impact and loss of trust.
State what changed (factually, without excuses).
Offer a corrective action with a deadline.
Log/track follow-through and confirm completion.
micro_script: I broke our agreement about ___.
I understand that impacts trust as ___.
Here is what I will do now: ___. By ___.

truth_repair_protocol.title: Truth Repair Protocol
truth_repair_protocol.purpose: Repair omission/half-truth/withholding by correcting record and restoring transparency.
steps: Correct the inaccurate/incomplete statement clearly.
Add the missing information that changes meaning.
Acknowledge impact of the distortion.
State the transparency rule for next time.
Offer verification (messages/logs) if helpful.

boundary_repair_protocol.title: Boundary Repair Protocol
boundary_repair_protocol.purpose: Repair boundary violations by stopping behavior and confirming limits going forward.
steps: Name the boundary that was violated.
Stop the violating behavior immediately.
Confirm understanding of the boundary.
State prevention plan (one concrete change).
Ask if anything else is needed to restore safety.

agreement_repair_protocol.title: Agreement Repair Protocol
agreement_repair_protocol.purpose: Repair moving goalposts/changing agreements by re-clarifying terms and expectations.
steps: Name that expectations changed.
Restate the original agreement.
Discuss whether it still stands and why.
Confirm a new agreement in one sentence.
Confirm follow-through checkpoints.

clarity_repair_protocol.title: Clarity Repair Protocol
clarity_repair_protocol.purpose: Repair exaggeration/overgeneralizing/assumption by returning to specifics and evidence.
steps: Replace always/never language with one concrete example.
Separate fact from interpretation.
Ask a clarifying question instead of assuming intent.
Restate the issue in precise, time-bounded language.
Confirm shared understanding.

focus_repair_protocol.title: Focus Repair Protocol
focus_repair_protocol.purpose: Repair deflection/avoidance by returning to the unresolved topic and closing the loop.
steps: Name the deflection.
Return to the original question/issue.
Answer directly first.
Then give context.
Confirm closure or next step.

resentment_repair_protocol.title: Resentment Repair Protocol
resentment_repair_protocol.purpose: Repair scorekeeping by returning to present issue and clean ownership.
steps: Name the scorekeeping pattern.
Return to the present issue only.
Own your part without listing their past failures.
Ask what repair is needed now.
If there are patterns, schedule a separate structured talk.

connection_repair_protocol.title: Connection Repair Protocol
connection_repair_protocol.purpose: Repair withholding affection by re-stabilizing warmth without coercion.
steps: Acknowledge warmth/affection was withdrawn as punishment.
Apologize for the relational threat signal.
Offer steady presence without bargaining.
Ask what helps connection feel safe (space vs closeness).
Commit to consistency.

dignity_repair_protocol.title: Dignity Repair Protocol
dignity_repair_protocol.purpose: Repair public shaming by restoring privacy, respect, and reputation where harmed.
steps: Acknowledge the public exposure/shaming.
Apologize directly for the dignity violation.
Correct misinformation publicly if needed.
Commit to privacy boundaries.
Offer a repair action to restore safety.

spiritual_repair_protocol.title: Spiritual Repair Protocol
spiritual_repair_protocol.purpose: Repair spiritual manipulation by separating faith from control and restoring humility.
steps: Name the misuse of scripture/spiritual authority.
Apologize for coercion/bypassing accountability.
Restate the issue without spiritual leverage.
Invite accountability from appropriate supports (pastor/mentor).
Commit to integrity in spiritual language.

direct_resolution_protocol.title: Direct Resolution Protocol
direct_resolution_protocol.purpose: Repair triangulation by returning to direct, respectful problem-solving.
steps: Name the triangulation (third party introduced for leverage).
Remove the third-party leverage from the discussion.
Return to direct dialogue with curiosity.
If mediation is needed, agree on a neutral process together.
Confirm next step and timeline.
